qa_pairs.question: What is OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenStack is a software suite used on clouds to control hardware resources like computers, storage, and networking.

qa_pairs.question: What core functionality does OpenStack provide regarding virtual machines?
qa_pairs.answer: It allows for the creation and management of virtual machines (VMs or instances) through software emulation.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of control does OpenStack offer over the computing environment?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenStack provides complete control over the computing environment, including choosing an operating system, software installation, and configuration.

qa_pairs.question: What are some examples of use cases supported by OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: Diverse use cases are supported, from hosting websites to creating virtual clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more documentation for OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: More documentation on OpenStack can be found at the OpenStack website (http://docs.openstack.org/).

qa_pairs.question: What must users understand before performing common tasks with OpenStack, according to this page?
qa_pairs.answer: It is assumed that users have already read 'Cloud Quick Start' and understand the basic operations of launching and connecting to a VM.

qa_pairs.question: What are the primary tools available for performing most tasks in OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: Most tasks can be performed using either the dashboard, the OpenStack command line clients, or a tool called Terraform.

qa_pairs.question: Are all OpenStack tasks manageable through the dashboard, command line, or Terraform?
qa_pairs.answer: No, some tasks require using command line tools, such as sharing an image with another project.

qa_pairs.question: What is the 'dashboard' in OpenStack terminology?
qa_pairs.answer: The dashboard refers to the web browser user interface used to manage your cloud resources.

qa_pairs.question: Which OpenStack sub-project is responsible for developing the dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: The dashboard is developed under an OpenStack sub-project referred to as Horizon.

qa_pairs.question: Can the terms 'Horizon' and 'dashboard' be used interchangeably?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Horizon and dashboard might be used interchangeably.

qa_pairs.question: What information can be found in the detailed documentation for the OpenStack dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: This documentation lists all of the options available on the dashboard, what they do, and how to navigate the system.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of OpenStack projects?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenStack projects group VMs together and provide a quota out of which VMs and related resources can be created.

qa_pairs.question: Are OpenStack projects unique across different clouds?
qa_pairs.answer: No, a project is unique to a particular cloud.

qa_pairs.question: What level of permissions do members of an OpenStack project have?
qa_pairs.answer: All accounts which are members of a project have the same level of permissions, meaning anyone can create or delete a VM within a project if they are a member.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user view the projects they are a member of?
qa_pairs.answer: You can view the projects you are a member of by logging into an OpenStack dashboard for the clouds you have access to.

qa_pairs.question: Where is the active project name displayed on the dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: The active project name will be displayed in the top left of the dashboard, to the right of the cloud logo.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user switch between multiple projects if they are a member of more than one?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are a member of more than one project, you can switch between active projects by clicking on the drop-down menu and selecting the project's name.

qa_pairs.question: Can project allocations limit the types of virtual machines that can be created?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, depending on your allocation, your project may be limited to certain types of VM flavors.

qa_pairs.question: What types of VM flavours are typically allowed by compute allocations and persistent allocations?
qa_pairs.answer: Compute allocations will generally only allow 'c' flavors, while persistent allocations will generally only allow 'p' flavors.

qa_pairs.question: Who is considered the owner of OpenStack projects?
qa_pairs.answer: Projects can be thought of as owned by primary investigators (PIs).

qa_pairs.question: Who can request new projects or adjustments to project quotas?
qa_pairs.answer: New projects and quota adjustments can only be requested by PIs.

qa_pairs.question: What is required for a user to gain access to an existing OpenStack project?
qa_pairs.answer: Request for access to an existing project must be confirmed by the PI owning the project.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more information about creating and managing storage volumes?
qa_pairs.answer: More information about creating and managing storage volumes can be found on the 'Working with volumes' page.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more information about creating and managing disk image files?
qa_pairs.answer: More information about creating and managing disk image files can be found on the 'Working with images' page.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more information about managing certain characteristics of their VMs in the dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: More information about managing certain characteristics of your VMs in the dashboard can be found on the 'Working with VMs' page.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of availability zones?
qa_pairs.answer: Availability zones allow you to indicate what group of physical hardware you would like your VM to run on.

qa_pairs.question: How many availability zones are available on Beluga and Graham clouds, and what is it called?
qa_pairs.answer: On Beluga and Graham clouds, there is only one availability zone, named 'nova'.

qa_pairs.question: How many availability zones are available on Arbutus, and what are their names?
qa_pairs.answer: On Arbutus there are three availability zones: 'Compute', 'Persistent_01', and 'Persistent_02'.

qa_pairs.question: What types of VM flavours are supported by the Compute and Persistent availability zones on Arbutus?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Compute' zones only run compute flavours, and the 'Persistent' zones only run persistent flavours, respectively.

qa_pairs.question: What is an advantage of using two persistent availability zones?
qa_pairs.answer: Using two persistent zones can present an advantage; for example, two instances of a website can run in two different zones to ensure its continuous availability in case one of the sites goes down.

qa_pairs.question: What is a security group in OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: A security group is a set of rules to control network traffic into and out of your virtual machines.

qa_pairs.question: How do you navigate to the security group management interface in OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: To manage security groups, go to 'Project->Network->Security Groups'.

qa_pairs.question: What will users see if they haven't defined any security groups previously?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have not previously defined any security groups, there will be a single default security group.

qa_pairs.question: How are rules added or removed from a security group?
qa_pairs.answer: To add or remove rules from a security group, click 'Manage Rules' beside that group. When the group description is displayed, you can add or remove rules by clicking the '+Add Rule' and 'Delete Rule' buttons.
